news.title: A tournament that crossed borders: crunching the numbers on the diaspora World Cup
news.lede: Nationality in football is increasingly complex and the influence of players from the diaspora has never been higher at a World Cup. “Nationality says where you come from. And I actually come from four countries: France, Algeria, Nigeria and Great Britain. I consider myself very lucky to possess these four parts, which all enrich me.”

news.title: The most unusual petition: they are collecting signatures in Argentina to have the final replayed!
news.lede: Argentina's defeat against Spain in the 2026 World Cup final continues to generate repercussions. In recent hours, a petition for FIFA to replay the tournament's last match has gained momentum, in which Spain won 1-0 after Ferran Torres' goal in extra time. The initiative, created by a fan identified as Gisela Sánchez, has already gathered thousands of signatures and has reignited the controversy surrounding the outcome of the championship. In recent hours, a petition on the Change.org platform has gained momentum, calling on FIFA to replay the match, considering that the "controversial and corrupt" performance of the Slovenian referee Slavko Vincic harmed La Albiceleste in decisive moments of the match.

news.title: Argentina Vs Spain World Cup Final Analysis | The myth surrounding Argentina’s victimhood
news.lede: Which team suffered the most at this World Cup? Ask that question and you'll quickly find yourself drowning in conspiracy theories. On one side stand the 2022 World Cup champions and three-time winners Argentina, proudly holding up pamphlets bearing the slogan "Us against the world" in bold red. On the other stand, well, the representatives of the other 47 teams at the tournament.

news.title: New faces, gulfs in class and flawed drama: how 48 teams changed the World Cup | Billy Munday
news.lede: The first 48-team World Cup had its positives and negatives – do we need to brace for a 64-team future? It was less than 12 months after Gianni Infantino succeeded Sepp Blatter as president that Fifa announced the expansion of the men’s World Cup from 32 to 48 teams. Diego Maradona said he was “delighted by Gianni’s initiative” because it allowed each country to dream of reaching the tournament, renewing their passion for football. Joachim Löw, then in charge of the world champions Germany, said: “I don’t think it’s a good idea to dilute the sporting value … In the long term, the quality suffers.”

news.title: Trossard, Ramos, Dumfries: 10 transfers you may have missed during the World Cup
news.lede: Too engrossed with the World Cup drama to keep up with goings-on in the transfer market? Announced just before England’s soul-sucking semi-final defeat against Argentina, it would have been easy to miss Trossard’s £15.3m move to Istanbul. While, after three and a half seasons and at the age of 31, it feels like his time at Arsenal has come to a natural conclusion, he will be remembered as a player who scored in clutch moments and, ultimately, played a crucial role in the club’s first league title in 22 years. Trossard departs after 174 appearances and 36 goals, a significant portion of which came from the bench in times of desperate need.

news.title: Between The Lines: World Cup could prove the grass really is greener for football
news.lede: The 2026 FIFA World Cup may leave a lasting legacy beyond soccer, and it could eventually impact both the NFL and CFL. Many stadiums that typically feature artificial turf installed natural grass to host World Cup matches, reigniting the debate over playing surfaces in professional football. During Sunday's FIFA World Cup final, San Francisco 49ers star tight end George Kittle took to social media, praising the natural grass at MetLife Stadium and urging the NFL to make the switch permanently. Arash Madani believes Kittle's comments reflect a conversation that is only going to grow louder.

news.title: Barcelona’s fans are not happy with the World Cup Team of the Tournament
news.lede: Supporters of La Liga giants Barcelona the world over have been left bemused by the announcement of the official 2026 World Cup Team of the Tournament. This comes owing to the absence of a leading Blaugrana star, namely Pau Cubarsí. Stopper Cubarsí is for his part of course fresh off nothing short of a spectacular individual campaign at the World Cup. From his berth at the heart of Spain’s backline, the 19-year-old barely put a foot wrong throughout the entire tournament.

news.title: Argentina football federation to consider legal action over 2026 FIFA World Cup misinformation
news.lede: The Argentine Football Association (AFA) is considering legal action over what it says was a coordinated misinformation campaign targeting the national team and its players during the 2026 FIFA World Cup. Days after Spain defeated Argentina to win the 2026 FIFA World Cup, a wave of anti-Argentina content flooded social media, with viral posts ranging from accusations of racism to alleged misconduct involving the AFA. Among the most widely shared claims were American actor Samuel L. Jackson’s description of Argentina as one of the “most racist countries in the world” and French journalist Romain Molina’s allegations against the AFA.
